clos.text: Apply Hess's law to compute standard enthalpy changes for reactions using tabulated standard enthalpies of formation.
Compute standard entropy changes for reactions using tabulated standard molar entropies and predict the sign from changes in gas-phase moles.
Calculate the standard Gibbs free energy change of a reaction from standard enthalpy and entropy changes and interpret its sign for spontaneity.
Compute non-standard Gibbs free energy using the reaction quotient and interpret deviations from standard conditions.
Relate the equilibrium constant to the standard Gibbs free energy change and predict whether products or reactants are favored.
Use the van't Hoff equation to predict how the equilibrium constant changes with temperature for endothermic and exothermic reactions.
Carry out thermodynamic calculations with consistent units, correctly converting between J and kJ and using temperature in Kelvin.

topics.name: Hess's law and standard enthalpies of formation
Standard entropy change and sign prediction
Standard Gibbs free energy and spontaneity
Non-standard Gibbs free energy and the reaction quotient
Equilibrium constant from Gibbs free energy
Van't Hoff equation and temperature dependence

guiding_principles: Emphasize quantitative reasoning over rote definitions. Items should require students to use formulas, not just recall them. Avoid items that depend on memorizing tabulated values; provide any needed values in the stem. Prefer items that test conceptual understanding alongside calculation: an item that asks students to predict the sign of a quantity before computing it is more valuable than one that asks only for the numeric answer. Penalize unit errors only when units are integral to the construct (e.g., J vs kJ when combining enthalpy and entropy).
